summary: DHH briefly notes a lunch conversation about legislation and personal choice that is causing him to shift his thinking. He describes moving from a pragmatic stance to a more idealistic approach on these interrelated issues. The post is essentially a teaser, promising more detailed thoughts to come. It captures an early moment of intellectual evolution in DHH's thinking.
key_points: DHH is reconsidering his positions on legislation and personal choice
He is shifting from pragmatism toward idealism on these issues
He sees legislation and personal choice as interconnected rather than separate topics
money_quotes: I'm actually in the process of changing my position on both (or actually it's somewhat the same) issues from a pragmative stance to a more idealistic approach.
key_insight: DHH signals an early intellectual shift from pragmatism to idealism on questions of legislation versus personal choice.
